text.title: generative-ui-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for generative-ui-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Accepting the first generated variant without comparing to alternatives.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single-variant acceptance forecloses the design space early, defeating the purpose of generative UI for exploration.

failure-mode.description: Merging generated output directly into production without manual review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generated code frequently contains API hallucinations, missing accessibility attributes, and composition violations that require manual cleanup.

failure-mode.description: Using Galileo or Uizard output as Figma-import-ready components.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both produce image-based outputs, not editable component trees; importing into Figma requires manual rebuild from scratch.

failure-mode.description: Using generative UI for design system contributions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generated components bypass token governance, variant structure, and composition rules; they require significant rework to meet design system standards.

failure-mode.description: Expecting AI-generated code to converge on novel interaction models.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents generating UI converge on modal patterns (cards, hero banners, nav bars) — novel interaction models are underrepresented in training data and rarely generated.

failure-mode.description: Assuming brand consistency will hold across multiple generation runs without anchoring.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Brand consistency degrades with each iteration; AI drifts from style guide without explicit anchoring via design tokens or reference images in prompts.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates code with library APIs that don't exist or are deprecated.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs hallucinate library APIs, especially for newer React versions and niche component libraries; always test generated code in a sandbox before accepting.

failure-mode.description: Treating WCAG compliance prompts as sufficient for accessible output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generated code that targets WCAG compliance often has broken focus order and missing live regions; axe-cli audit is mandatory regardless of prompt instructions.

failure-mode.description: Using "modern" or "clean" as style constraints without concrete references.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Abstract style terms mean different things every run; add specific style references (typography system, component library names, example URLs) for reproducible output.

failure-mode.description: Accepting agent-generated complex component trees without checking against design system composition rules.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents propose component nesting that violates design system composition rules silently; the output looks correct but breaks at the system level.
